rulings.Case Title: MARTIFER-SILVERADO FUND I, LLC VS. TALESUN SOLAR USA LTD
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for February 9, 2026, line 1. PLAINTIFF MARTIFER-SILVERADO FUND I, LLC'S MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE. The unopposed motion to admit Charlene C. Sun pro hac vice is granted. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for February 9, 2026, line 2. CROSS DEFENDANT AMERICAN CRICKET ENTERPRISES LLC'S Amended DEMURRER to Amended CROSS COMPLAINT. rulings.Case Title: SARKIS GEGAMYAN VS. ATLASSIAN US, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents (Set Two) And Special Interrogatories (Set Two)
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for February 9, 2026, line 3. DEFENDANT ATLASSIAN US, INC.'S Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents (Set Two) And Special Interrogatories (Set Two). Defendant's motion to compel Plaintiff's responses to its requests for production of documents, set two, and special interrogatories, set two, is granted. Plaintiff has failed to timely respond to the discovery and has waived objections. (See Code Civ. Proc., sec. 2030.300, subd. (a); id., sec. 2031.300, subd. (a).) Plaintiff is ordered to provide complete, verified, objection-free responses by February 23, 2026. Plaintiff has filed an opposition that raises his self-represented status. There is no stay of discovery while a party is self-represented, and the law applies equally to self-represented litigants. Plaintiff also raises objections to the discovery requests. By failing to timely respond, he has waived objections and must provide substantially compliant responses in order to file a motion with the court seeking relief from waiver. A party who files a lawsuit takes on an obligation to provide information to the other side about his claims upon a proper discovery request Sanctions are mandatory against a party who unsuccessfully opposes a discovery motion unless the court finds that the party acted with substantial justification or other circumstances make imposition of the sanction unjust. (Code Civ. Proc., sec. 2030.290, subd. (c); id., sec. 2031.300, subd. (c).) The court does not find substantial justification but imposes a nominal sanction of $50, which Plaintiff shall pay to Defendant by March 9, 2026. The court cautions Plaintiff that further violations of his discovery obligations could lead to further sanctions, including increased monetary sanctions or evidentiary, issue, or terminating sanctions. Defendant shall provide notice of entry of the order to Plaintiff. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: SARKIS GEGAMYAN VS. ATLASSIAN US, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendants Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents (Set Two) And Special Interrogatories (Set One)
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for February 9, 2026, line 3. PLAINTIFF SARKIS GEGAMYAN'S Motion To Compel Defendants Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents (Set Two) And Special Interrogatories (Set One). Plaintiff's motion to compel defendant's responses to discovery is denied. The motion is untimely, because it was filed and served fewer than 16 court days in advance of the hearing date (plus additional time depending on the method of service). (See Code Civ. Proc., sec. 1005, subd. (b).) In addition, a party who receives a discovery request has 30 days to respond (plus additional time depending on the method of service). (Code Civ. Proc., sec. 2031.260, subd. (a).) Defendant's responses are not yet due pursuant to the Code. (Carradero Decl., para. 2 [electronic service on Jan. 21, 2026].) For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)
